events.name: Robo Soccer
events.description: An exciting robotics competition where teams design and build autonomous robots to play soccer. Showcase your engineering and programming skills as your robot competes against others in this fast-paced tournament.
rules: Teams must consist of 4-5 members
Robots must not exceed 20cm x 20cm x 20cm dimensions
Robot weight limit: 2kg
All robots must be autonomous (no remote control)
Battery-powered robots only
Match duration: 10 minutes per game
Best 2 out of 3 matches wins the round
Technical specifications must be submitted 3 days before the event
events.eligibility: Students from any year and branch
events.judging: Based on robot performance, design innovation, and programming logic

events.name: AI Video Challenge
events.description: Create innovative video content that demonstrates AI applications in real-world scenarios. Use machine learning, computer vision, or any AI technique to produce a compelling 3-5 minute video. Show creativity, technical depth, and presentation quality.
rules: Video duration: 3-5 minutes
Video format: MP4, MOV, or AVI (1080p minimum)
Must showcase AI/ML application
Original content required (no plagiarism)
Team size: 2-3 members
Submission deadline: March 18, 2026
Videos will be judged on creativity, technical implementation, and content quality
Multiple submissions per team not allowed
events.judging: Expert panel including AI researchers and industry professionals

events.name: Mad Ads
events.description: Unleash your creativity! Design an engaging advertisement campaign for a fictional or real product. The twist? Create content using unique themes and unconventional approaches. Present your complete campaign including visuals, taglines, and marketing strategy.
rules: Team size: 3-4 members
Presentation time: 8-10 minutes
Must include: visual design, tagline, and marketing strategy
Can use any medium: digital, print, or multimedia
No offensive or inappropriate content
Originality is key - unique concepts will be favored
Use of existing brand guidelines is not allowed for fictional products
Judges' decision is final
events.eligibility: Open to all students interested in marketing and creative design
events.judging: Based on creativity, originality, execution, and presentation quality

events.name: Buildspace
events.description: A 3-day hackathon where teams build innovative software or hardware solutions to real-world problems. Work with mentors, leverage APIs and tools, and pitch your idea at the end. The most impactful and feasible solution wins.
rules: Team size: 2-4 members
Event duration: 3 days (continuous or extended hours)
Original code/design required - no pre-built projects
Open to all technology stacks and frameworks
Teams must submit working prototype
Final presentation: 5 minutes pitch + 2 minutes Q&A
Collaboration tools and APIs provided
Mentors available for guidance throughout the event
events.eligibility: All college students, no experience requirement
events.judging: Technical feasibility, innovation, implementation, and business potential
whatIncluded: Access to development tools and APIs

timeline.day1: Team formation, ideation, and development kickoff
